name: Barbarian
description: Storm with Rage, and wade into hand-to-hand combat. Then follow the Path of the Berserker to unleash raw violence.

startingEquipment.description: Greataxe, 4 Handaxes, Explorer's Pack, and 15 GP

features.name: Rage
features.description: In battle, you fight with primal ferocity. On your turn, you can enter a rage as a Bonus Action. While raging, you gain Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws, bonus rage damage on melee weapon attacks using Strength, and Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. Your rage lasts for 1 minute.

features.name: Unarmored Defense
features.description: While you are not wearing any armor, your Armor Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency.

choices.name: Weapon Mastery
choices.description: Choose 2 weapons you have proficiency with.

features.name: Danger Sense
features.description: You gain an uncanny sense of when things nearby aren't as they should be. You have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws. You don't gain this benefit if you have the Incapacitated condition.

features.name: Reckless Attack
features.description: You can throw aside all concern for defense to attack with fierce desperation. When you make your first attack on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly, giving you Advantage on melee weapon attack rolls using Strength during this turn, but attack rolls against you have Advantage until your next turn.

features.name: Barbarian Subclass
features.description: You gain a Barbarian subclass of your choice. The Path of the Berserker subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Primal Knowledge
features.description: You gain proficiency in another skill from the Barbarian skill list.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Fast Movement
features.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor.

features.name: Feral Instinct
features.description: Your instincts are so honed that you have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

features.name: Instinctive Pounce
features.description: As part of the Bonus Action you take to enter your rage, you can move up to half your speed.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Brutal Strike
features.description: If you use Reckless Attack, you can forgo Advantage on the next attack roll you make on your turn with a Strength-based attack. If that attack hits, the target takes an extra 1d10 damage and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked Prone or pushed 15 feet away from you.

features.name: Relentless Rage
features.description: Your rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 Hit Points while raging and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, your Hit Points instead change to a number equal to twice your Barbarian level. Each time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5. The DC resets when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Improved Brutal Strike
features.description: You have honed new ways to attack furiously. When using Brutal Strike, you can choose Hamstring Blow (reducing the target's Speed by 15 feet until the start of your next turn) or Staggering Blow (the target has Disadvantage on its next saving throw before the end of your next turn).

features.name: Persistent Rage
features.description: Your rage is so fierce that it ends early only if you fall Unconscious or if you choose to end it. In addition, if you start your turn with no hostile creature within 60 feet, your rage doesn't end.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Improved Brutal Strike
features.description: Your Brutal Strike damage increases to 2d10. In addition, you can use two different Brutal Strike effects whenever you use your Brutal Strike feature.

features.name: Indomitable Might
features.description: If your total for a Strength check or Strength saving throw is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Primal Champion
features.description: You embody the power of the wilds. Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4. Your maximum for those scores is now 24.

subclasses.name: Path of the Berserker
subclasses.description: For some Barbarians, rage is a means to an end — that end being violence. The Path of the Berserker is a path of untrammeled fury, slick with blood.

features.name: Frenzy
features.description: If you use Reckless Attack while your rage is active, you deal extra damage to the first target you hit on your turn with a Strength-based attack. The extra damage equals your Rage Damage bonus. In addition, when you use your Reckless Attack, you can make one extra attack as part of the same action.

features.name: Mindless Rage
features.description: You can't be Charmed or Frightened while raging. If you are Charmed or Frightened when you enter your rage, the effect is suspended for the duration of the rage.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: When you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can use your Reaction to make one melee attack against that creature, using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike.

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can strike terror into others with your menacing presence. When you do, each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Strength modifier). On a failed save, a creature has the Frightened condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of the Frightened creature's turns, the creature repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.

name: Bard
description: Perform spells that inspire and heal allies or beguile foes. Then join the College of Lore to collect knowledge and magical secrets.

startingEquipment.description: Leather Armor, 2 Daggers, Entertainer's Pack, and 19 GP

features.name: Bardic Inspiration
features.description: You can inspire others through words, music, or dance. As a Bonus Action, you can give one Bardic Inspiration die (1d6) to a creature within 60 feet who can hear you. The creature can add this die to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes within 10 minutes. The Bardic Inspiration die increases to 1d8 at level 5, 1d10 at level 10, and 1d12 at level 15. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), regaining all uses when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to cast spells through your study of music and performance. See the Bard spell list.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

choices.name: Expertise
choices.description: Choose 2 skill proficiencies to gain expertise in.

features.name: Jack of All Trades
features.description: You can add half your Proficiency Bonus (round down) to any ability check you make that doesn't already use your Proficiency Bonus.

features.name: Bard Subclass
features.description: You gain a Bard subclass of your choice. The College of Lore subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Font of Inspiration
features.description: You now regain all of your expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

features.name: Countercharm
features.description: You can use musical notes or words of power to disrupt mind-influencing effects. If you or a creature within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw against an effect that applies the Charmed or Frightened condition, you can use your Reaction to grant a reroll. The new roll must be used.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

choices.name: Expertise
choices.description: Choose 2 skill proficiencies to gain expertise in.

features.name: Magical Secrets
features.description: You have plundered magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any spell list. The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Superior Inspiration
features.description: When you roll Initiative, you regain two expended uses of Bardic Inspiration.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Words of Creation
features.description: You have mastered two of the prime Words of Creation: the words of life and death. You always have Power Word Heal and Power Word Kill prepared. When you cast either spell, you can target a second creature within 10 feet of the first with the same spell, using the same spell slot.

subclasses.name: College of Lore
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Lore know something about most things, collecting bits of knowledge from sources as diverse as scholarly tomes and peasant tales.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.

features.name: Cutting Words
features.description: You learn how to use your wit to distract, confuse, and otherwise sap the confidence of others. When a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a damage roll, you can use your Reaction to expend one of your Bardic Inspiration dice, rolling it and subtracting the number rolled from the creature's roll.

features.name: Magical Discoveries
features.description: You learn two spells from any spell list. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast. The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you.

features.name: Peerless Skill
features.description: When you make an ability check or attack roll and fail, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration, roll the Bardic Inspiration die, and add the number rolled to the d20, potentially turning a failure into a success. On a failed ability check, the Inspiration is not expended.

name: Cleric
description: Invoke divine magic to heal, bolster, and smite. Then harness the Life Domain to be a master of healing.

startingEquipment.description: Chain Shirt, Shield, Mace, Holy Symbol (Amulet), Priest's Pack, and 7 GP

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to cast spells through prayer and meditation. See the Cleric spell list.

features.name: Divine Order
features.description: You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice: Protector (you gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Heavy armor) or Thaumaturge (you gain one extra cantrip from the Cleric spell list and add your Wisdom modifier to Intelligence (Religion) checks).

choices.name: Divine Order
choices.description: Choose your divine order.

options.name: Protector
options.description: You gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Heavy armor.

options.name: Thaumaturge
options.description: You gain one extra Cleric cantrip and add your Wisdom modifier to Intelligence (Religion) checks.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You can channel divine energy directly from the Outer Planes to fuel magical effects. You start with two Channel Divinity effects: Divine Spark and Turn Undead. Each time you use Channel Divinity, you choose which effect to create. You can use Channel Divinity twice and regain all expended uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest. You gain an additional use at level 6 (3 total) and level 18 (4 total).

features.name: Cleric Subclass
features.description: You gain a Cleric subclass of your choice. The Life Domain subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Sear Undead
features.description: Whenever you use Turn Undead, you can roll a number of d8s equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1d8) and add the rolls together. Each Undead that fails its saving throw against your Turn Undead takes Radiant damage equal to the roll's total. This damage increases to d12s at level 14.

features.name: Blessed Strikes
features.description: Divine power infuses you in battle. When a creature takes damage from one of your cantrips or weapon attacks, you can also deal 1d8 Radiant damage to that creature. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Divine Intervention
features.description: You can call on your deity to intervene on your behalf. As a Magic action, choose any Cleric spell of level 5 or lower that doesn't require a Reaction to cast. As part of the same action, you cast that spell without expending a spell slot or needing Material components. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Improved Blessed Strikes
features.description: The extra damage of your Blessed Strikes increases to 2d8.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Greater Divine Intervention
features.description: You can call on even more powerful divine intervention. When you use Divine Intervention, you can choose any spell from the Cleric spell list, including level 6-9 spells. Once you use this enhanced feature, you can't use Divine Intervention again until you finish 2d4 Long Rests.

subclasses.name: Life Domain
subclasses.description: The Life Domain focuses on the vibrant positive energy — one of the fundamental forces of the multiverse — that sustains all life.

features.name: Disciple of Life
features.description: When a spell you cast with a spell slot restores Hit Points to a creature, that creature regains additional Hit Points on the turn you cast the spell. The additional Hit Points equal 2 + the spell's level.

features.name: Life Domain Spells
features.description: Your connection to the Life Domain ensures you always have certain spells ready: at level 3 you gain Aid, Bless, Cure Wounds, and Lesser Restoration; higher levels add more healing spells.

features.name: Preserve Life
features.description: As a Magic action, you present your Holy Symbol and evoke healing energy that can restore a number of Hit Points equal to five times your Cleric level. Choose any creatures within 30 feet of you, and divide those Hit Points among them. This feature can restore a creature to no more than half of its Hit Point maximum.

features.name: Blessed Healer
features.description: The healing spells you cast on others heal you as well. When you cast a spell with a spell slot that restores Hit Points to a creature other than you, you regain Hit Points equal to 2 + the spell's level.

features.name: Supreme Healing
features.description: When you would normally roll one or more dice to restore Hit Points with a spell, you instead use the highest number possible for each die.

name: Druid
description: Channel nature magic to heal, shape-shift, and control the elements. Then join the Circle of the Land to draw on the magic of the environment.

startingEquipment.description: Leather Armor, Shield, Sickle, Druidic Focus (Wooden Staff), Explorer's Pack, Herbalism Kit, and 9 GP

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: Drawing on the divine essence of nature, you can cast spells to shape that essence to your will. See the Druid spell list.

features.name: Druidic
features.description: You know Druidic, the secret language of Druids. You can use it to leave hidden messages and you always have the Speak with Animals spell prepared.

features.name: Primal Order
features.description: You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice: Magician (you know one extra Druid cantrip and add your Wisdom modifier to Intelligence (Nature) checks) or Warden (you gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Medium armor).

choices.name: Primal Order
choices.description: Choose your primal order.

options.name: Magician
options.description: You know one extra Druid cantrip and add your Wisdom modifier to Intelligence (Nature) checks.

options.name: Warden
options.description: You gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Medium armor.

features.name: Wild Shape
features.description: The power of nature allows you to assume the form of an animal. As a Bonus Action, you can shape-shift into a Beast form. You stay in that form for a number of hours equal to half your Druid level (round down), and you can use Wild Shape a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining all uses when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Wild Companion
features.description: You can summon a nature spirit. You can expend a use of Wild Shape to cast Find Familiar without Material components. When you cast it this way, the familiar is a Fey and disappears when you use this feature again or when a number of hours equal to half your Druid level have passed.

features.name: Druid Subclass
features.description: You gain a Druid subclass of your choice. The Circle of the Land subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Wild Resurgence
features.description: If you have no uses of Wild Shape left, you can give yourself one use by expending a spell slot (no action required). You can do so only once per turn. In addition, you can expend one use of Wild Shape (no action required) to give yourself a level 1 spell slot, and you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Elemental Fury
features.description: The might of the elements flows through you. You gain one of the following options of your choice: Potent Spellcasting (you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any Druid cantrip) or Primal Strike (once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can deal an extra 1d8 Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage).

choices.name: Elemental Fury Option
choices.description: Choose one Elemental Fury option.

options.name: Potent Spellcasting
options.description: Add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any Druid cantrip.

options.name: Primal Strike
options.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit with a weapon or Unarmed Strike attack, you can deal an extra 1d8 Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Improved Elemental Fury
features.description: The option you chose for Elemental Fury grows more powerful. Potent Spellcasting: the extra damage from this feature is now 2d8. Primal Strike: the extra damage is now 2d8.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Beast Spells
features.description: While in a Beast form from Wild Shape, you can cast spells. You can provide Verbal, Somatic, and Material components for the spells you cast.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Archdruid
features.description: The vitality of nature constantly blooms within you, granting you the following benefits: you regain one expended use of Wild Shape whenever you start your turn with 0 uses remaining, and you can cast Alter Self at will without expending a spell slot.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Land
subclasses.description: The Circle of the Land is made up of mystics and sages who safeguard ancient knowledge and rites through oral tradition. These Druids meet in sacred circles of trees or standing stones to whisper primal secrets in Druidic.

features.name: Circle of the Land Spells
features.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, choose one type of land: arid, polar, temperate, or tropical. Consult the associated list of Circle spells. You always have those spells prepared.

features.name: Land's Aid
features.description: As a Magic action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape and choose a point within 60 feet of you. Vitality-giving flowers and life-draining thorns appear for a moment in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point. Each creature of your choice in that area regains Hit Points equal to 2d6 plus your Wisdom modifier, and each creature of your choice in that area must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC, taking 2d6 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. The healing and damage increase by 1d6 at Druid levels 10 and 14.

features.name: Natural Recovery
features.description: You can cast one of your Circle spells without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a Long Rest before doing so again. At Druid level 10, you gain a second use of this feature.

features.name: Nature's Ward
features.description: You are immune to the Poisoned condition, and you have Resistance to a damage type associated with your current land choice.

features.name: Nature's Sanctuary
features.description: When a creature within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, you can use your Reaction to force the attacker to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the attacker must target a different creature or lose the attack. On a successful save, the attacker is immune to this effect for 24 hours.

name: Fighter
description: Master all weapons and armor. Then embody the Champion to strive for peak combat prowess.

startingEquipment.description: Chain Mail, Greatsword, Flail, 8 Javelins, Dungeoneer's Pack, and 4 GP

features.name: Fighting Style
features.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one Fighting Style feat.

choices.name: Fighting Style
choices.description: Choose a Fighting Style feat.

options.name: Archery
options.description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with Ranged weapons.

options.name: Defense
options.description: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

options.name: Dueling
options.description: When you are wielding a Melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

options.name: Great Weapon Fighting
options.description: When you roll damage for an attack you make with a Melee weapon that you are holding with two hands, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a damage die as a 3. The weapon must have the Two-Handed or Versatile property to gain this benefit.

options.name: Protection
options.description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to interpose your Shield. You impose Disadvantage on the attack roll.

options.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
options.description: When you make an extra attack as a result of using a weapon that has the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack.

features.name: Second Wind
features.description: You have a limited well of stamina. As a Bonus Action, you can regain Hit Points equal to 1d10 + your Fighter level. You can use this feature twice and regain all expended uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest. You gain an additional use at levels 4 and 10.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of three kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency.

choices.name: Weapon Mastery
choices.description: Choose 3 weapons you have proficiency with.

features.name: Action Surge
features.description: You can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. On your turn, you can take one additional action. Once you use this feature, you must finish a Short or Long Rest before you can use it again. You gain a second use at level 17.

features.name: Tactical Mind
features.description: You have a mind for tactics. When you fail an ability check, you can expend a use of your Second Wind, rolling 1d10 and adding the number rolled to the ability check, potentially turning a failure into a success. If the check still fails, the Second Wind use isn't expended.

features.name: Fighter Subclass
features.description: You gain a Fighter subclass of your choice. The Champion subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Tactical Shift
features.description: Whenever you activate your Second Wind with a Bonus Action, you can move up to half your speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Indomitable
features.description: If you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it with a bonus equal to your Fighter level divided by 3 (round down), and you must use the new roll. You can use this feature twice and gain a third use at level 13.

features.name: Tactical Master
features.description: When you attack with a weapon whose mastery property you can use, you can replace that property with the Push, Sap, or Slow property for that attack.

features.name: Two Extra Attacks
features.description: You can attack three times instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Indomitable (three uses)
features.description: You can now use Indomitable three times between Long Rests.

features.name: Studied Attacks
features.description: You study your opponents and learn from each attack you make. If you make an attack roll against a creature and miss, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Action Surge (two uses)
features.description: You can use Action Surge twice before a rest, but only once per turn.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Three Extra Attacks
features.description: You can attack four times instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

subclasses.name: Champion
subclasses.description: A Champion focuses on the development of martial prowess in a relentless pursuit of victory. Champions combine rigorous training with physical excellence to deal devastating blows.

features.name: Improved Critical
features.description: Your attack rolls with weapons and Unarmed Strikes can score a Critical Hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20.

features.name: Remarkable Athlete
features.description: Thanks to your athleticism, you have Advantage on Initiative rolls and Strength (Athletics) checks. In addition, immediately after you score a Critical Hit, you can move up to half your Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

features.name: Additional Fighting Style
features.description: You gain another Fighting Style feat of your choice.

choices.name: Additional Fighting Style
choices.description: Choose a Fighting Style feat.

options.name: Archery
options.description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with Ranged weapons.

options.name: Defense
options.description: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

options.name: Dueling
options.description: When you are wielding a Melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

options.name: Great Weapon Fighting
options.description: When you roll damage for an attack you make with a Melee weapon that you are holding with two hands, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a damage die as a 3. The weapon must have the Two-Handed or Versatile property to gain this benefit.

options.name: Protection
options.description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to interpose your Shield. You impose Disadvantage on the attack roll.

options.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
options.description: When you make an extra attack as a result of using a weapon that has the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack.

features.name: Heroic Warrior
features.description: The thrill of battle drives you toward victory. During combat, you can give yourself Heroic Inspiration whenever you start your turn without it.

features.name: Superior Critical
features.description: Your attack rolls with weapons and Unarmed Strikes can now score a Critical Hit on a roll of 18-20 on the d20.

features.name: Survivor
features.description: You attain the pinnacle of resilience in battle. Defy Death: you have Advantage on Death Saving Throws, and when you roll 18-20 on a Death Saving Throw, you gain the benefit of rolling a 20. Heroic Rally: at the start of each of your turns, you regain Hit Points equal to 5 + your Constitution modifier if you are Bloodied and have at least 1 Hit Point.

name: Monk
description: Dart in and out of melee while striking fast and hard. Then become a Warrior of the Open Hand to master unarmed combat.

startingEquipment.description: Spear, 5 Daggers, Explorer's Pack, and 11 GP

features.name: Martial Arts
features.description: Your practice of martial arts gives you mastery of combat styles that use Unarmed Strikes and Monk weapons (Simple Melee weapons and Martial Melee weapons with the Light property). You gain Bonus Unarmed Strike (as a Bonus Action), Martial Arts Die (1d6, increasing at higher levels), and Dexterous Attacks (you can use Dexterity for attack and damage rolls with Unarmed Strikes and Monk weapons).

features.name: Unarmored Defense
features.description: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, your base Armor Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier.

features.name: Monk's Focus
features.description: Your focus and martial training allow you to harness extraordinary energy: Focus Points. Your Monk level determines your number of points. You can expend these points to fuel Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind. You regain all expended Focus Points when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

features.name: Unarmored Movement
features.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield. This bonus increases as you gain Monk levels, as shown in the Monk Features table.

features.name: Uncanny Metabolism
features.description: When you roll Initiative, you can regain all expended Focus Points. When you do so, roll your Martial Arts die and regain a number of Hit Points equal to your Monk level plus the number rolled. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Deflect Attacks
features.description: When an attack roll hits you and its damage includes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can take a Reaction to reduce the attack's total damage against you. The reduction equals 1d10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Monk level. If you reduce the damage to 0, you can expend 1 Focus Point to redirect some of the attack's force.

features.name: Monk Subclass
features.description: You gain a Monk subclass of your choice. The Warrior of the Open Hand subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Slow Fall
features.description: You can take a Reaction when you fall to reduce any damage you take from the fall by an amount equal to five times your Monk level.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Stunning Strike
features.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a Monk weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can expend 1 Focus Point to attempt a stunning strike. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the Stunned condition until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Empowered Strikes
features.description: Whenever you deal damage with your Unarmed Strike, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.

features.name: Evasion
features.description: When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail. You don't benefit from this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Acrobatic Movement
features.description: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the movement.

features.name: Heightened Focus
features.description: Your Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind gain the following improvements: Flurry of Blows now lets you make three Unarmed Strikes with it for 1 Focus Point; Patient Defense grants Temporary Hit Points equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die; Step of the Wind gives a Fly Speed for the current turn.

features.name: Self-Restoration
features.description: Through sheer force of will, you can remove one of the following conditions from yourself at the end of each of your turns: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned. In addition, forgoing food and drink doesn't give you levels of Exhaustion.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Deflect Energy
features.description: You can now use your Deflect Attacks feature against attacks that deal any damage type, not just Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing.

features.name: Disciplined Survivor
features.description: Your physical and mental discipline grant you proficiency in all saving throws. Additionally, whenever you make a saving throw and fail, you can expend 1 Focus Point to reroll it, and you must use the new roll.

features.name: Perfect Focus
features.description: When you roll Initiative and don't use Uncanny Metabolism, you regain expended Focus Points until you have 4 if you have fewer than 4.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Superior Defense
features.description: At the start of your turn, you can expend 3 Focus Points to bolster yourself against harm for 1 minute or until you have the Incapacitated condition. During that time, you have Resistance to all damage except Force damage.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Body and Mind
features.description: You have developed your body and mind to new heights. Your Dexterity and Wisdom scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

subclasses.name: Warrior of the Open Hand
subclasses.description: Warriors of the Open Hand are masters of unarmed combat. They learn techniques to push and trip their opponents and manipulate their own energy to protect themselves from harm.

features.name: Open Hand Technique
features.description: Whenever you hit a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects: Addle (the target can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn), Push (the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away), or Topple (the target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the Prone condition).

features.name: Wholeness of Body
features.description: You gain the ability to heal yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can roll your Martial Arts die. You regain a number of Hit Points equal to the number rolled plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Fleet Step
features.description: When you take a Bonus Action other than Step of the Wind, you can also use Step of the Wind immediately after that Bonus Action.

features.name: Quivering Palm
features.description: You gain the ability to set up lethal vibrations in someone's body. When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you can expend 4 Focus Points to start these imperceptible vibrations, which last for a number of days equal to your Monk level. You can have only one creature under the effect at a time. When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can end the vibrations harmlessly or force the target to make a Constitution saving throw, taking 10d12 Force damage on a failed save or half on a success.

name: Paladin
description: Smite foes and shield allies with divine and martial might. Then swear the Oath of Devotion to emulate the angels of justice.

startingEquipment.description: Chain Mail, Shield, Longsword, 6 Javelins, Holy Symbol (Amulet), Priest's Pack, and 9 GP

features.name: Lay On Hands
features.description: Your blessed touch can heal wounds. You have a pool of healing power that replenishes when you finish a Long Rest. With that pool, you can restore a total number of Hit Points equal to five times your Paladin level.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to cast spells through prayer and meditation. See the Paladin spell list.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency.

choices.name: Weapon Mastery
choices.description: Choose 2 weapons you have proficiency with.

features.name: Fighting Style
features.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one Fighting Style feat.

choices.name: Fighting Style
choices.description: Choose a Fighting Style feat.

options.name: Archery
options.description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with Ranged weapons.

options.name: Defense
options.description: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

options.name: Dueling
options.description: When you are wielding a Melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

options.name: Great Weapon Fighting
options.description: When you roll damage for an attack you make with a Melee weapon that you are holding with two hands, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a damage die as a 3. The weapon must have the Two-Handed or Versatile property to gain this benefit.

options.name: Protection
options.description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to interpose your Shield. You impose Disadvantage on the attack roll.

options.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
options.description: When you make an extra attack as a result of using a weapon that has the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack.

features.name: Paladin's Smite
features.description: You always have the Divine Smite spell prepared. In addition, you can cast it without expending a spell slot, but you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You can channel divine energy to fuel magical effects. Each Channel Divinity option provided by your subclass explains how to use it. You can use this feature twice and regain all expended uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

features.name: Paladin Subclass
features.description: You gain a Paladin subclass of your choice. The Oath of Devotion subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Faithful Steed
features.description: You can cast Find Steed without expending a spell slot. You can also cast it normally. When you cast it either way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Aura of Protection
features.description: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you gain a bonus to saving throws equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum +1). This aura extends to 30 feet at level 18.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Abjure Foes
features.description: As a Magic action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to present your Holy Symbol and speak a prayer of denunciation. Each creature of your choice within 60 feet must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature has the Frightened condition for 1 minute. The Frightened creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns.

features.name: Aura of Courage
features.description: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can't be Frightened while you are conscious. This aura extends to 30 feet at level 18.

features.name: Radiant Strikes
features.description: Your strikes carry divine power. When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a Melee weapon or an Unarmed Strike, the target takes an extra 1d8 Radiant damage.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Restoring Touch
features.description: When you use Lay on Hands on a creature, you can also remove one or more of the following conditions from the creature: Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Frightened, Paralyzed, or Stunned. You must expend 5 Hit Points from the healing pool for each condition removed.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Aura Expansion
features.description: Your Aura of Protection and Aura of Courage now extend to 30 feet.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

subclasses.name: Oath of Devotion
subclasses.description: The Oath of Devotion binds a Paladin to the loftiest ideals of justice, virtue, and order. These Paladins meet the ideal of the knight in shining armor.

features.name: Oath of Devotion Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath gives you the following spells. They are always prepared and count as Paladin spells for you.

features.name: Sacred Weapon
features.description: When you take the Attack action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to imbue one Melee weapon that you are holding with positive energy. For 10 minutes, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls made with that weapon (minimum +1). The weapon also emits Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. The effect ends if you no longer hold the weapon or are Incapacitated.

features.name: Aura of Devotion
features.description: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can't be Charmed while you are conscious. This aura extends to 30 feet at Paladin level 18.

features.name: Smite of Protection
features.description: Your divine smite becomes a form of protection. When any creature within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your Reaction to expend a spell slot and reduce the damage by 2d8 plus 1d8 per spell level above 1.

features.name: Holy Nimbus
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can emanate an aura of sunlight for 1 minute. The aura sheds Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet. Whenever an enemy creature starts its turn in the Bright Light, it takes 10 Radiant damage. You also have Advantage on saving throws against spells cast by Fiends or Undead.

name: Ranger
description: Weave together martial prowess, nature magic, and survival skills. Then embody the Hunter to protect nature with martial versatility.

startingEquipment.description: Studded Leather Armor, Scimitar, Shortsword, Longbow, 20 Arrows, Druidic Focus (Sprig of Mistletoe), Explorer's Pack, and 7 GP

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to channel the magical essence of nature to cast spells. See the Ranger spell list.

features.name: Favored Enemy
features.description: You always have the Hunter's Mark spell prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare. You can cast it a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier without expending a spell slot, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency.

choices.name: Weapon Mastery
choices.description: Choose 2 weapons you have proficiency with.

features.name: Deft Explorer
features.description: Thanks to your travels, you gain the following benefits: you gain Expertise in one of your skill proficiencies, and you can speak, read, and write two languages of your choice.

choices.name: Deft Explorer Expertise
choices.description: Choose one skill proficiency to gain Expertise in.

options.name: Sleight of Hand
options.description: Gain Expertise in Sleight of Hand.

features.name: Fighting Style
features.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one Fighting Style feat.

choices.name: Fighting Style
choices.description: Choose a Fighting Style feat.

options.name: Archery
options.description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with Ranged weapons.

options.name: Defense
options.description: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

options.name: Dueling
options.description: When you are wielding a Melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

options.name: Great Weapon Fighting
options.description: When you roll damage for an attack you make with a Melee weapon that you are holding with two hands, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a damage die as a 3. The weapon must have the Two-Handed or Versatile property to gain this benefit.

options.name: Protection
options.description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to interpose your Shield. You impose Disadvantage on the attack roll.

options.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
options.description: When you make an extra attack as a result of using a weapon that has the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack.

features.name: Ranger Subclass
features.description: You gain a Ranger subclass of your choice. The Hunter subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Roving
features.description: Your walking speed increases by 10 feet. You also gain a Climb Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your walking speed.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in one of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

choices.name: Expertise
choices.description: Choose 1 skill proficiency to gain expertise in.

features.name: Tireless
features.description: Primal forces now help fuel you on every foray. You have a number of d8s equal to your Proficiency Bonus. Whenever you finish a Short Rest, you can spend one of those dice, roll it, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the roll plus your Wisdom modifier.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Relentless Hunter
features.description: Taking damage can't break your Concentration on Hunter's Mark.

features.name: Nature's Veil
features.description: You invoke spirits of nature to magically hide yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can become Invisible until the start of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Precise Hunter
features.description: You have Advantage on attack rolls against the creature currently marked by your Hunter's Mark.

features.name: Feral Senses
features.description: Your connection to the forces of nature grants you Blindsight with a range of 30 feet.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Foe Slayer
features.description: You become an unparalleled hunter of your enemies. Once per turn when you hit a creature marked by your Hunter's Mark, you can deal extra damage equal to your Wisdom modifier to that creature. You can also use this feature on a miss to turn it into a hit instead.

subclasses.name: Hunter
subclasses.description: You stalk prey in the wilds and elsewhere, using your abilities to protect the world against threats.

features.name: Hunter's Lore
features.description: You can call on the forces of nature to reveal certain strengths and weaknesses of your prey. While a creature is marked by your Hunter's Mark, you know whether that creature has any Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities, and if so, what they are.

features.name: Hunter's Prey
features.description: You gain one of the following features of your choice. Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can replace the chosen option with the other one.

choices.name: Hunter's Prey
choices.description: Choose one of the following features.

options.name: Colossus Slayer
options.description: Your tenacity can wear down even the most resilient foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon, the weapon deals an extra 1d8 damage to the target if it's missing any of its Hit Points. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.

options.name: Horde Breaker
options.description: Once on each of your turns when you make an attack with a weapon, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target, that is within the weapon's range, and that you haven't attacked this turn.

features.name: Defensive Tactics
features.description: You gain one of the following features of your choice. Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can replace the chosen option with the other one.

choices.name: Defensive Tactics
choices.description: Choose one of the following features.

options.name: Escape the Horde
options.description: Opportunity Attacks have Disadvantage against you.

options.name: Multiattack Defense
options.description: When a creature hits you with an attack roll, that creature has Disadvantage on all other attack rolls against you this turn.

features.name: Superior Hunter's Prey
features.description: Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature marked by your Hunter's Mark, you can also deal that spell's extra damage to a different creature that you can see within 30 feet of the first creature.

features.name: Superior Hunter's Defense
features.description: When you take damage, you can take a Reaction to give yourself Resistance to that damage and any other damage of the same type until the end of the current turn.

name: Rogue
description: Launch deadly Sneak Attacks while avoiding harm through stealth. Then embody the Thief to master infiltration and treasure hunting.

startingEquipment.description: Leather Armor, 2 Daggers, Shortsword, Shortbow, 20 Arrows, Thieves' Tools, Burglar's Pack, and 8 GP

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

choices.name: Expertise
choices.description: Choose 2 skill proficiencies to gain expertise in.

features.name: Sneak Attack
features.description: You know how to strike subtly and exploit a foe's distraction. Once per turn, you can deal extra damage to one creature you hit with an attack roll if you have Advantage on the roll and the attack uses a Finesse or Ranged weapon. The extra damage is listed in the Sneak Attack column of the Rogue Features table.

features.name: Thieves' Cant
features.description: You know Thieves' Cant and one other language of your choice. You can use Thieves' Cant to hide messages in seemingly normal conversation. Only another creature that knows Thieves' Cant understands such messages.

choices.name: Language
choices.description: Choose one Standard language.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency.

choices.name: Weapon Mastery
choices.description: Choose 2 weapons you have proficiency with.

features.name: Cunning Action
features.description: Your quick thinking and agility allow you to act speedily. You can take a Bonus Action on each of your turns in combat to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

features.name: Rogue Subclass
features.description: You gain a Rogue subclass of your choice. The Thief subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Steady Aim
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you give yourself Advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn. You can use this feature only if you haven't moved during this turn, and after you use it, your Speed is 0 until the end of the current turn.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Cunning Strike
features.description: You have developed cunning ways to use your Sneak Attack. When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can add one of the following effects: Disarm (the target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or drop one item it's holding), Poison (you deal extra Poison damage equal to your Sneak Attack damage, and the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the Poisoned condition for 1 minute), or Trip (the target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the Prone condition).

features.name: Uncanny Dodge
features.description: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can use your Reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

choices.name: Expertise
choices.description: Choose 2 skill proficiencies to gain expertise in.

features.name: Evasion
features.description: When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail. You don't benefit from this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition.

features.name: Reliable Talent
features.description: Whenever you make an ability check that uses a skill proficiency, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Improved Cunning Strike
features.description: You can use up to two Cunning Strike effects when you deal Sneak Attack damage, paying the cost for each effect.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Devious Strikes
features.description: You gain additional Cunning Strike options: Daze (the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the Dazed condition until the end of your next turn), Knock Out (the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the Unconscious condition for 1 minute; the condition ends early if the target takes damage), and Obscure (you become Invisible until the end of your next turn).

features.name: Slippery Mind
features.description: You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Elusive
features.description: You are so evasive that attackers rarely gain the upper hand against you. No attack roll has Advantage against you unless you have the Incapacitated condition.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Stroke of Luck
features.description: You have an uncanny knack for succeeding when you need to. If you fail a d20 Test, you can turn the roll into a 20. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

subclasses.name: Thief
subclasses.description: A Thief hones the skills of stealth and infiltration. Burglars, bandits, cutpurses, and other criminals typically follow this archetype, though so do Rogues who prefer to think of themselves as professional treasure seekers.

features.name: Fast Hands
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can do one of the following: make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check, use Thieves' Tools to try to disarm a trap or open a lock, or take the Use an Object action.

features.name: Second-Story Work
features.description: You have trained to reach especially hard-to-reach places. You gain a Climb Speed equal to your Speed. In addition, when you make a running jump, the distance you cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Dexterity modifier.

features.name: Supreme Sneak
features.description: You have Advantage on every Dexterity (Stealth) check you make, provided you aren't wearing Medium or Heavy armor.

features.name: Use Magic Device
features.description: You have learned enough about the workings of magic that you can improvise the use of items even when they are not intended for you. You ignore all class, species, and level requirements on the use of magic items.

features.name: Thief's Reflexes
features.description: You have become adept at laying ambushes and quickly escaping danger. You can take two turns during the first round of any combat. You take your first turn at your normal Initiative and your second turn at your Initiative minus 10.

name: Sorcerer
description: Wield magic innate to your being, shaping the power to your will. Then channel Draconic Sorcery to breathe the magic of dragons.

startingEquipment.description: Spear, 2 Daggers, Arcane Focus (Crystal), Dungeoneer's Pack, and 28 GP

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: An event in your past or in the life of a parent or ancestor left an indelible mark on you, granting you the ability to cast spells. See the Sorcerer spell list.

features.name: Innate Sorcery
features.description: An event in your past left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with simmering magic. As a Bonus Action, you can unleash that magic for 1 minute, during which you gain Advantage on the attack rolls of Sorcerer spells you cast and your spell save DC increases by 1. You can use this feature twice and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Font of Magic
features.description: You can tap into a deep wellspring of magic within yourself represented by Sorcery Points. You have a number of Sorcery Points equal to your Sorcerer level. You can use Sorcery Points to gain additional spell slots or sacrifice spell slots to gain additional Sorcery Points.

features.name: Metamagic
features.description: You gain the ability to twist your spells to suit your needs. You learn two Metamagic options of your choice. You learn additional options at levels 10 and 17. When you cast a spell, you can use one Metamagic option, expending the Sorcery Points it requires.

choices.name: Metamagic
choices.description: Choose 2 Metamagic options.

options.name: Careful Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, choose a number of those creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). Each chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Distant Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell with a range of 5 feet or greater, you can double the range. When you cast a spell with a range of Touch, you can change the range to 30 feet. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Empowered Spell
options.description: When you roll damage for a spell, you can reroll a number of damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). You must use the new rolls. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Extended Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell with a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can double its duration to a maximum of 24 hours. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Heightened Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw, you can give one target of the spell Disadvantage on saves against the spell. Cost: 3 Sorcery Points.

options.name: Quickened Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell with a casting time of an Action, you can change the casting time to a Bonus Action. Cost: 2 Sorcery Points.

options.name: Seeking Spell
options.description: When you make an attack roll for a spell and miss, you can reroll the d20 and must use the new roll. Cost: 2 Sorcery Points.

options.name: Subtle Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell, you can cast it without any Verbal or Somatic components. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Transmuted Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that deals a type of damage from the following list, you can change that damage type to one of the other listed types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Thunder. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Twinned Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that targets only one creature and doesn't have a range of Self, you can target a second creature in range with the same spell. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point per spell level (1 for cantrips).

features.name: Sorcerer Subclass
features.description: You gain a Sorcerer subclass of your choice. The Draconic Sorcery subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Sorcerous Restoration
features.description: When you finish a Short Rest, you can regain expended Sorcery Points, but no more than a number equal to half your Sorcerer level (round down). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Sorcery Incarnate
features.description: While your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you can use up to two Metamagic options on each spell you cast. In addition, if you have no uses of Innate Sorcery left, you can use it if you spend 2 Sorcery Points when you take the Bonus Action to activate it.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Metamagic
features.description: You learn an additional Metamagic option.

choices.name: Metamagic
choices.description: Choose 1 Metamagic option.

options.name: Careful Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, choose a number of those creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). Each chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Distant Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell with a range of 5 feet or greater, you can double the range. When you cast a spell with a range of Touch, you can change the range to 30 feet. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Empowered Spell
options.description: When you roll damage for a spell, you can reroll a number of damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). You must use the new rolls. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Extended Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell with a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can double its duration to a maximum of 24 hours. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Heightened Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw, you can give one target of the spell Disadvantage on saves against the spell. Cost: 3 Sorcery Points.

options.name: Quickened Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell with a casting time of an Action, you can change the casting time to a Bonus Action. Cost: 2 Sorcery Points.

options.name: Seeking Spell
options.description: When you make an attack roll for a spell and miss, you can reroll the d20 and must use the new roll. Cost: 2 Sorcery Points.

options.name: Subtle Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell, you can cast it without any Verbal or Somatic components. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Transmuted Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that deals a type of damage from the following list, you can change that damage type to one of the other listed types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Thunder. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point.

options.name: Twinned Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that targets only one creature and doesn't have a range of Self, you can target a second creature in range with the same spell. Cost: 1 Sorcery Point per spell level (1 for cantrips).

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.
