features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Arcane Apotheosis
features.description: You can use one Metamagic option on each of your turns without expending Sorcery Points on it.

subclasses.name: Draconic Sorcery
subclasses.description: Your innate magic comes from the gift of a dragon. Perhaps an ancient dragon befriended a distant ancestor and bestowed a portion of their power, or you might have absorbed magic from being exposed to a dragon's breath.

features.name: Draconic Resilience
features.description: The magic in your body manifests physical traits of your draconic ancestor. Your Hit Point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 again whenever you gain a level in this class. Parts of your skin are covered by a thin sheen of scales. When you aren't wearing armor, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Charisma modifier.

features.name: Draconic Spells
features.description: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Draconic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Elemental Affinity
features.description: Your draconic magic has an affinity with a damage type associated with dragons. Choose one type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. You have Resistance to that damage type, and when you cast a spell that deals damage of that type, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

features.name: Dragon Wings
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can cause draconic wings to appear on your back. The wings last for 1 hour or until you dismiss them. You have a Fly Speed of 60 feet while the wings are present. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you spend 3 Sorcery Points to restore it.

features.name: Dragon Companion
features.description: You can cast Summon Dragon without a Material component. You can also cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a Long Rest. Whenever you cast it, you can modify it so it doesn't require Concentration and its duration becomes 1 minute.

name: Warlock
description: Cast spells derived from occult knowledge. Then form a pact with the Fiend Patron to call on sinister powers.

startingEquipment.description: Leather Armor, Sickle, 2 Daggers, Arcane Focus (Orb), Scholar's Pack, and 15 GP

features.name: Eldritch Invocations
features.description: You have unearthed Eldritch Invocations, pieces of forbidden knowledge that imbue you with an abiding magical ability. You gain one invocation of your choice, such as Pact of the Tome. When you gain certain Warlock levels, you gain more invocations as shown in the Warlock Features table.

choices.name: Eldritch Invocations
choices.description: Choose 1 Eldritch Invocation.

options.name: Agonizing Blast
options.description: When you cast Eldritch Blast, add your Charisma modifier to the damage it deals on a hit.

options.name: Armor of Shadows
options.description: You can cast Mage Armor on yourself without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Ascendant Step
options.description: You can cast Levitate on yourself without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Beast Speech
options.description: You can cast Speak with Animals at will, without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Beguiling Influence
options.description: You gain proficiency in Deception and Persuasion.

options.name: Devil's Sight
options.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 120 feet. You can see normally in magical and nonmagical Darkness.

options.name: Eldritch Mind
options.description: You have Advantage on Constitution saving throws to maintain Concentration.

options.name: Eldritch Spear
options.description: When you cast Eldritch Blast, its range is 300 feet.

options.name: Fiendish Vigor
options.description: You can cast False Life on yourself without expending a spell slot. When you do so, you gain the spell's maximum number of Temporary Hit Points.

options.name: Gaze of Two Minds
options.description: You can use a Bonus Action to touch a willing Humanoid and perceive through their senses.

options.name: Gift of the Depths
options.description: You can breathe underwater, and you have a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

options.name: Investment of the Chain Master
options.description: Your familiar gains additional benefits when you have the Pact of the Chain.

options.name: Lessons of the First Ones
options.description: You gain one feat of your choice that lacks prerequisites.

options.name: Mask of Many Faces
options.description: You can cast Disguise Self at will, without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Master of Myriad Forms
options.description: You can cast Alter Self at will, without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Misty Visions
options.description: You can cast Silent Image at will, without expending a spell slot.

options.name: One with Shadows
options.description: When you are in an area of Dim Light or Darkness, you can take a Magic action to become Invisible until you move, take an action, or take a reaction.

options.name: Otherworldly Leap
options.description: You can cast Jump on yourself without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Pact of the Blade
options.description: As a Bonus Action, you can conjure a pact weapon in your empty hand or bond with a magic weapon. You can use your Charisma modifier for attack and damage rolls with this weapon.

options.name: Pact of the Chain
options.description: You learn the Find Familiar spell and can cast it as a Magic action without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Pact of the Tome
options.description: Your patron gives you a Book of Shadows. Choose three cantrips from any spell list. While the book is on your person, you can cast those cantrips at will.

options.name: Repelling Blast
options.description: When you hit a creature with Eldritch Blast, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from you.

options.name: Thirsting Blade
options.description: You can attack with your pact weapon twice instead of once when you take the Attack action on your turn.

options.name: Whispers of the Grave
options.description: You can cast Speak with Dead at will, without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Witch Sight
options.description: You have Truesight with a range of 30 feet.

features.name: Pact Magic
features.description: Through occult ceremony, you have formed a pact with a mysterious entity to gain magical powers. You have spell slots that you regain when you finish a Short or Long Rest. All your Pact Magic spell slots are the same level.

features.name: Magical Cunning
features.description: You can perform an esoteric rite for 1 minute. At the end of it, you regain expended Pact Magic spell slots but no more than a number equal to half your maximum (round up). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Warlock Subclass
features.description: You gain a Warlock subclass of your choice. The Fiend Patron subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Contact Patron
features.description: You always have the Contact Other Plane spell prepared. You can cast it without expending a spell slot to contact your patron, and you automatically succeed on the spell's saving throw.

features.name: Mystic Arcanum
features.description: Your patron bestows upon you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one level 6 spell from the Warlock spell list as this arcanum. You can cast this spell once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a Long Rest before you can do so again. You gain additional arcana at levels 13 (level 7), 15 (level 8), and 17 (level 9).

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Mystic Arcanum (level 7 spell)
features.description: Choose one level 7 Warlock spell as your arcanum. You can cast it once without expending a spell slot, regaining the ability when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Mystic Arcanum (level 8 spell)
features.description: Choose one level 8 Warlock spell as your arcanum. You can cast it once without expending a spell slot, regaining the ability when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Mystic Arcanum (level 9 spell)
features.description: Choose one level 9 Warlock spell as your arcanum. You can cast it once without expending a spell slot, regaining the ability when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Eldritch Master
features.description: When you use your Magical Cunning feature, you regain all your expended Pact Magic spell slots.

subclasses.name: Fiend Patron
subclasses.description: Your pact draws on the Lower Planes, the realms of perdition. You might forge a bargain with a demon lord, an archdevil, or another fiend. That patron's aims are evil — the corruption or destruction of all things — and your path is defined by the extent to which you strive against those aims.

features.name: Dark One's Blessing
features.description: When you reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Charisma modifier plus your Warlock level (minimum 1 Temporary Hit Point). You also gain this benefit if someone else reduces an enemy within 10 feet of you to 0 Hit Points.

features.name: Fiend Spells
features.description: The magic of your patron ensures certain spells are ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the Fiend Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Dark One's Own Luck
features.description: You can call on your fiendish patron to alter fate in your favor. When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can use this feature to add 1d10 to your roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Fiendish Resilience
features.description: Choose one damage type, other than Force. Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you have Resistance to that damage type until you choose a different one with this feature.

features.name: Hurl Through Hell
features.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can try to instantly transport the target through the Lower Planes. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC, or the target disappears and hurtles through a nightmare landscape. It takes 8d10 Psychic damage if it isn't a Fiend and has the Incapacitated condition until the end of your next turn.

name: Wizard
description: Study arcane magic and master spells for every purpose. Then embody the Evoker to create explosive effects.

startingEquipment.description: 2 Daggers, Arcane Focus (Staff), Spellbook, Scholar's Pack, and 5 GP

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: As a student of arcane magic, you have learned to cast spells. See the Wizard spell list.

features.name: Ritual Adept
features.description: You can cast any spell as a Ritual if that spell has the Ritual tag and the spell is in your spellbook. You needn't have the spell prepared, but you must read from the book to cast it this way.

features.name: Arcane Recovery
features.description: You can regain some of your magical energy by studying your spellbook. When you finish a Short Rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to no more than half your Wizard level (round up), and none of the slots can be level 6 or higher. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Scholar
features.description: While studying magic, you also specialized in another field of study. Choose one of the following skills in which you have proficiency: Arcana, History, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, or Religion. You have Expertise in the chosen skill.

choices.name: Scholar Expertise
choices.description: Choose 1 skill to gain Expertise in.

features.name: Wizard Subclass
features.description: You gain a Wizard subclass of your choice. The Evoker subclass is detailed after this class's description.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Memorize Spell
features.description: Whenever you finish a Short Rest, you can study your spellbook and replace one of the level 1+ Wizard spells you have prepared for your Spellcasting feature with another level 1+ spell from the book.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Spell Mastery
features.description: You have achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast them at will. Choose a level 1 and a level 2 spell in your spellbook that have a casting time of an action. You always have those spells prepared, and you can cast them at their lowest level without expending a spell slot. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Signature Spells
features.description: Choose two level 3 spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each of them once at level 3 without expending a spell slot. When you do so, you can't cast them this way again until you finish a Short or Long Rest. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot.

subclasses.name: Evoker
subclasses.description: Your studies focus on magic that creates powerful elemental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid.

features.name: Evocation Savant
features.description: Choose two Wizard spells from the Evocation school, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots, you can add one Wizard spell from the Evocation school to your spellbook for free.

features.name: Potent Cantrip
features.description: Your damaging cantrips affect even creatures that avoid the brunt of the effect. When a creature succeeds on a saving throw against your cantrip, the creature takes half the cantrip's damage (if any) but suffers no additional effect from the cantrip.

features.name: Sculpt Spells
features.description: You can create pockets of relative safety within the effects of your evocations. When you cast an Evocation spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to 1 + the spell's level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and they take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a success.

features.name: Empowered Evocation
features.description: Whenever you cast a Wizard spell from the Evocation school, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

features.name: Overchannel
features.description: You can increase the power of your spells. When you cast a Wizard spell with a spell slot of levels 1-5 that deals damage, you can deal maximum damage with that spell on the turn you cast it. The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. Each subsequent time you use this feature before finishing a Long Rest, you take 2d12 Necrotic damage per spell level immediately after casting. This damage ignores Resistance and Immunity.
